comment: POSITIVE redaction fixtures. Each entry tests one detector. Synthetic strings only — none are real credentials. The high-signal prefixes (`sk-`, `xoxb-`, `AKIA`, `ghp_`, `glpat-`) are split into `prefix` + `body` so the committed JSON never contains the literal pattern that GitHub's secret scanner would flag. The test concatenates them at runtime to form `value`.

cases.label: aws-access-key
cases.why: Spec §5.9 detector: 'AKIA' prefix. Body is the canonical AWS docs example.

cases.label: jwt-three-segments
cases.why: Spec §5.9 detector: JWT (three base64url segments separated by dots)

cases.label: labeled-context-hex-token-equals
cases.why: Spec §5.9 labeled-context boundary rule — hex 32+ chars next to a label MUST be redacted

cases.label: labeled-context-hex-token-colon
cases.why: Spec §5.9 labeled-context boundary rule — labeled context with colon

cases.label: labeled-context-bearer
cases.why: Spec §5.9 labeled-context boundary rule — Bearer header
